title: Unknown Input, Zod 4 Validation, Domain Types, and Output Contracts · Contract Defense
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for unknown input, zod 4 validation, domain types, and output contracts and demands the smallest safe runtime schema and trusted domain boundary with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for runtime schema and trusted domain boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model body, params, query, fields, configuration, token claims, database rows, and upstream data as unknown until runtime validation succeeds.
Use Zod 4 strict objects, safeParse, issue paths, bounds, unions, refinements, and error mapping without accepting undeclared properties.
Order decoding, validation, canonicalization, authorization, and effect so normalization cannot change the resource or policy target after checks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Unknown Before Validation · changed case · Quiz Unknown Before Validation
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 7616 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay pressure. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to admit unknown input through strict runtime and output schemas; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then abort during a dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model body, params, query, fields, configuration, token claims, database rows, and upstream data as unknown until runtime validation succeeds.
